mainmatter.p: Let a \in A be an element of the dcpo A. To show that \eta _A a is positive, we fix a semidirected subset U \subseteq \mathsf { L } {A} such that \eta _A a \sqsubseteq \bigsqcup U to show that U is inhabited. The inequality implies that \top \sqsubseteq \exists u \in U. u{ \downarrow }, so we know that U is inhabited.

Conversely, suppose that u \in \mathsf { L } {A} is a positive element. Let U be the semidirected family { \mathopen {} \left \{ v \mid v = u \land u \downarrow \right \} \mathclose {}}; we evidently have u \sqsubseteq \bigsqcup {U}, so U is inhabited. Thus it follows that u is defined.

frontmatter.title: Positivity = definedness in a lifted dcpo

mainmatter.p: The positive elements of a lifted dcpo \mathsf { L } {A} are exactly the defined elements, i.e. those lying in the image of { A } \xrightarrow {{ \eta }}{ \mathsf { L } {A} }.
